title: Sticking around the subforum and or EGF for 30 Stuffs
display_title: Sticking around the subforum and or EGF for 30 Stuffs
wikitext:
[]: Sticking around the subforum and/or EGF for 3.0 Stuffs?             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Tue Nov 13, 2012 1:27 pm I have seen some people comment that they will not be frequenting this forum after November 17th, in order to not get spoiled with the events in Rebuild 3.0 until they have had a chance to watch the movie themselves. I count myself among this group. Spoiler policies notwithstanding, I will probably limit myself to not looking at the "Rebuild of Evangelion" subforum until such time as I have had the opportunity to watch the movie (with subtitles), which may take several months to a year. I may or may not frequent the other subforums. So this is a temporary goodbye from me, as you will be seeing a lot less of my posts from this weekend onward. (I will, however, post one last compiled list of predictions for the 3.0 movie before the 17th.) I warmly invite other people with similar sentiments to use this thread for similar notifications.

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Tue Nov 13, 2012 1:31 pm FYI, there is a dedicated thread for this in Off-Topic.

[]:             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Tue Nov 13, 2012 1:32 pm esselfortium wrote: FYI, there is a dedicated thread for this in Off-Topic.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Tue Nov 13, 2012 1:35 pm Jornophelanthas wrote: Really? I never check there.

[]:             Quote Post by Dima » Tue Nov 13, 2012 1:38 pm Well we can always make a Spoilers Thread to avoid these kind of problems.

[]:             Quote Post by Rj123541 » Tue Nov 13, 2012 1:38 pm Dima wrote: Well we can always make a Spoilers Thread to avoid these kind of problems.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Nov 13, 2012 2:05 pm This is probably a suitable time to remind people that all talk about the movie as it is actually shown in theatres shall be placed in marked spoiler tags, and will continue that way at least until the first home video release and the inevitable fansubs that will hit the 'net shortly thereafter.

[]:             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Tue Nov 13, 2012 2:16 pm Thank you Mr. Tines for the reminder. I am aware of that policy and thankful for it. My personal spoiler worry, however, is mostly on the formulation of thread titles. Even if this is moderated, some revealing thread title (e.g. "OMG Shinji/Kaworu/Gendo dies/survives/merges with Lilith!!!!") could slip through the cracks, and it is simply impossible for this to be redacted within seconds. Regardless, until I have seen the movie itself, I hardly expect to have much to contribute in the discussion of the Rebuild movies, as the third installment has every chance to invalidate current interpretations of the first two movies.

[]:             Quote Post by Lonecow12 » Tue Nov 13, 2012 2:29 pm We need a Thread titled: Does Asuka die? and then a Yes or No and then lock the thread. That's the only thing I want to be spoiled on. I don't need any more details then that.

[]:             Quote Post by Rj123541 » Tue Nov 13, 2012 2:38 pm Lonecow12 wrote: We need a Thread titled: Does Asuka die? and then a Yes or No and then lock the thread. That's the only thing I want to be spoiled on. I don't need any more details then that.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Tue Nov 13, 2012 2:42 pm I'm with Jornophelanthas on this one. I'll be limiting my time on EGF as well. The spoiler policy is nice, but I can't be sure how many people will follow it faithfully. There's also the risk of trolls, and as OP said they can't all be redacted in time.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Tue Nov 13, 2012 4:57 pm Too bad for you guys, personally I'm there specifically for the spoilers, I'm starting to count the hours that separate us from this experience.

[]:             Quote Post by Fireball » Tue Nov 13, 2012 5:31 pm Will be hard to avoid 4chan spoilers. I know that shit will be everywhere, but I won't comment or read until I've seen it either. (not like I'm posting much on this section anyway )

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Tue Nov 13, 2012 5:52 pm I spoil myself all the time (books, movies, anime), so I won't be absenting myself from looking.

[]:             Quote Post by Stan » Tue Nov 13, 2012 6:37 pm Well from the many forums I participate in, I would say the EVAGEEKS forum is rather respectful to each other and doesn't sear, curse or spoil anything without a warning for the most part. I think the fact that the community is so respectful deserves praise. So don't worry there will be spoiler tags. But I do understand your concern. Personally I expect a fan sub sometime soon so there wont be years of waiting like with 2.0 before seeing it.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Nov 13, 2012 7:19 pm On the 16th I'll watch the 6 minute 3.0-TV preview then on the 17th I'll read Ornette's spoilerless 3.0 review (e.g. http://www.evageeks.org/2009/06/my-spoilerless-evangelion-2-0-review/ ) but besides that, I'll avoid both the Rebuild subforum and our Rizon IRC channel #egf-rebuild. Still not sure if I'm holding out for a crappy cam-rip or glorious BD-rip, but I hope to pre-order the Japanese 3.33 BD when it'll be available.

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Tue Nov 13, 2012 7:20 pm Stan wrote: But I do understand your concern. Personally I expect a fan sub sometime soon so there wont be years of waiting like with 2.0 before seeing it.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Tue Nov 13, 2012 7:28 pm How does that first 7mins or so preview of film count in terms of spoiler policy? I think that should count as freely discussed material just like trailers etc.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Nov 13, 2012 7:36 pm essel: I thought the translation process took a bit longer than a month. Xard wrote: How does that first 7mins or so preview of film count in terms of spoiler policy? I think that should count as freely discussed material just like trailers etc.

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Tue Nov 13, 2012 8:07 pm Reichu wrote: essel: I thought the translation process took a bit longer than a month.
